verdicts.note: Quote 'Modalities: eeg, beh' appears verbatim in e2 and lists 'eeg' first, supporting eeg as the primary modality.
Value is correctly left null with no evidence asserted; the repository record and README indeed contain no clinical/disease-status statement, so no unsupported claim is made.
Quote 'DatasetDOI: doi:10.18112/openneuro.ds008037.v1.0.0' appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes the DOI value.
Quote 'License: CC0' appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes the license value.
Quote 'Number of subjects: 123' appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes the subject count.
Quote 'OpenNeuro dataset ds008037 (snapshot 1.0.0)' appears verbatim in e2 and establishes OpenNeuro as the provider.
Value is correctly left null with no evidence asserted; no site/institution location appears anywhere in the record.
Value is correctly left null with no evidence asserted; the word 'human' never appears in the record, matching the drafter's own caveat.

The only cited quote, 'License: CC0', establishes the copyright license, not the data-access mechanism. CC0 is a permissive license but does not itself state that the dataset is openly/freely accessible without restriction (e.g., no registration, no embargo) — that is a distinct claim the quote does not make. This conflates license with access status.
Value is correctly left null with no evidence asserted; no access/download terms beyond the license line appear in the record.
Value is correctly left null with no evidence asserted; no pricing statement appears in the record.

The long README quote appears verbatim in e2 (matches the source text exactly, truncated before the unrelated Structural-MRI sentence) and enumerates each data type/task listed in the value array. The 'Modalities: eeg, beh' quote is also verbatim and supports the presence of behavioural (beh) data.

All three cited quotes ('License: CC0', 'Number of subjects: 123', and the long README passage) appear verbatim in e2 and jointly support the facts stated. Describing CC0 as 'open (CC0)' is a reasonable characterization of the license itself (CC0 is a public-domain/maximally-permissive license), distinct from the stronger categorical 'access: open' claim made in the access field.

Same underlying quotes as short_desc_en, verified verbatim in e2; this is a translation of the same supported facts, with 'abierto (CC0)' being the same reasonable license characterization noted above.

Value is correctly left null; the 'needs' text accurately identifies that 'Created: 2026-06-26T01:54:51.365Z' (present verbatim in e2) is an upload/snapshot timestamp, not a data-collection period, so no unsupported year claim is made.
